page: Vultr vx1-m-192c-1536gb is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 192 shared AMD vCPUs and 1435.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 7.47 GB of RAM per vCPU. The instance does not include a dedicated GPU.

Storage is limited to a 1 GB network-attached volume, requiring external network storage solutions for data-intensive operations. The instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. A key architectural tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation, which means compute resources are shared with other tenants, potentially introducing resource contention under sustained heavy workloads.

Due to the shared CPU model, this server offers a high density of memory and vCPUs at a lower qualitative cost profile compared to dedicated-core instances. It is designed for memory-intensive workloads that can tolerate shared CPU performance, such as large-scale caching layers, in-memory databases, and offline data processing tasks that require substantial RAM but do not demand constant, dedicated single-core compute performance.
description: Vultr vx1-m-192c-1536gb is an x86_64 server instance in the VX1 Cloud Compute family, configured with 192 shared AMD vCPUs and 1435.0 GB of RAM. This configuration provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 7.47 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage, relying instead on a 1 GB network-attached volume, and contains no GPU accelerators. Because the CPU allocation is shared, the instance offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-heavy workloads that do not require dedicated compute cores. It is suitable for large-memory applications, in-memory databases, and caching layers that operate within a shared-resource environment.
og_description: Vultr vx1-m-192c-1536gb is an x86_64 server featuring 192 shared AMD vCPUs, 1435.0 GB of RAM, and a 1 GB network storage volume, designed for memory-intensive workloads in a shared-resource environment.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-m-192c-1536gb features 192 shared AMD vCPUs, 1435.0 GB RAM, and 1 GB network storage, offering a cost-efficient, memory-dense profile.
tagline: A high-capacity, memory-dense virtual server utilizing shared AMD processors and network-attached storage for large-scale memory workloads.

1435.0 GB of system memory providing 7.47 GB per core
Shared CPU allocation for qualitative cost efficiency
Best suited for in-memory databases and large-scale caching
